message:
fix(patterns): clear tail buffer when resetting detection context

Fix clear_seen() to also clear the tail_buffer, ensuring a truly fresh
detection context when requested.

Previously, clear_seen() only cleared the deduplication state (seen_keys,
seen_order) but left the tail_buffer intact. This could cause unexpected
behavior where patterns spanning the old tail buffer and new input would
still be detected, despite the caller expecting a complete reset.

The tail_buffer is used for cross-segment pattern matching in streaming
scenarios. When a user calls clear_seen(), they expect subsequent detect()
calls to behave as if starting fresh, which now includes the overlap
matching state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
